review_text: I’m going to watch a special trilogy showing of lord of the rings extended edition soon at my local theater. Can’t wait to see the battle of Helms Deep on a massive screen for the first time.
Simply the best trilogy out there. Hands down.
Fantastical adventure across Middle-Earth at its finest!

As Jackson himself acknowledged, it is difficult to create the second part of a trilogy for it has no real beginning or end (its purpose to pick up where the first movie ended, and set up the final instalment), yet The Two Towers delivers spectacularly, and then some. For all the repeat accolades from the first movie, enthralling action, emotional storytelling, and excellent performances from a stellar cast, it is the introduction of the Andy Serkis' Gollum that steals the show. Created using pioneering motion capture technology, Gollum feels eerily real. A complex, sinister, yet oddly charming character adds another layer of depth to this evolving narrative. Overall, an exceptional piece of filmmaking.
The Battle of Helms Deep is peak.
What are we holding on to, Sam? That there's some good in this world, Mr. Frodo, and it's worth fighting for. 🫶

Without a doubt, one of the best sequels, along with The Empire Strikes Back. It's true that the film again takes liberties with the original. This time, it shows us one of the great factions of good, their strengths, and their way of life. On screen, you'll witness the greatest siege ever filmed in the history of humankind; nothing like it will ever be filmed again. All its scenes are epic and relevant, making this film a work that will transcend time. Thank you for existing.

The fellowship is separated; Frodo and Sam continue heading to Mordor with Gollum as their guide, Merry and Pippin befriend an Ent, Legolas and Aragon reunite with Gandalf the White to free Rohan of Saruman’s curse. The groups continues towards Mordor after the Battle of Helm’s Deep and the Last March of the Ents. Each separated party has a memorable storyline that made this movie awesome.
arguably the best if the trilogy

Middle films of a trilogy are generally weaker than the other two because its transitional between the beginning promise and the ending dramatic conclusion (sometimes with a coda). This succeeds brilliantly because of the quality of both the story and the filming. The editing suits the need — more complicated and with several stories unlike the linearity of the opening. My one qualification — which I have not reflected in the rating — is that the longer-in-time shots compared with those in the opening — after having viewed the film gazillions of times — make this repetition a bit boring, sobtgatcevem the incredible Helms Deep sequence loses a bit of luster.

Thank You! Thank You! Thank You! Being able to see the Fellowship & the Two Towers in theaters once again was so amazing! Not only did I feel like a kid again seeing these films but it brought back so many memories & all the feelings as to why I fell in love with Tolkiens World in the first place!! Seeing everyone in the theater be just as excited as we were for the movie made my heart explode! If it weren’t for the Snow Storm we would have finished the Trilogy but THANK YOU again for the new memories & great times! LOTR FOREVER!
